questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider the following metallurgical processes.
(i) Heating impure metal with CO and distilling the resulting volatile carbonyl (b.p. $43^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ ) and finally decomposition at $150^{\circ}-200^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to get the pure metal
(ii) Heating the sulphide ore in air until a part is converted to oxide and then further heating in the absence of air to let the oxide react with unchanged metal sulphide
(iii) Electrolysis of the molten electrolyte containing approximately equal amounts of the metal chloride and NaCl to obtained the metal
"The processes used for obtaining magnesium, nickel and copper are respectively :
questions.solution:
(i) $\underset{\text { (impure) }}{\mathrm{Ni}}+4 \mathrm{CO} \xrightarrow{50^{\circ} \mathrm{C}} \underset{\text { (volatile) }}{\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{CO}_4\right)\right]} \xrightarrow{230^{\circ} \mathrm{C}} \underset{\text { (pure) }}{\mathrm{Ni}}+4 \mathrm{CO} \uparrow$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { (ii) } \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{~S}+\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{O}_2 \xrightarrow{\Delta} \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{SO}_2 \\ & \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{~S}+2 \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{O} \xrightarrow[\text { high }]{\text { high }} 6 \mathrm{Cu}+\mathrm{SO}_2\end{aligned}$


(iii) $\mathrm{MgCl}(\mathrm{s}) \xrightarrow{\text { electrolysis }} \mathrm{Mg}^{2+}(1)+2 \mathrm{Cl}$ (l)

At cathode : $\mathrm{Mg}^{2+}+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{s})$
At anode : $2 \mathrm{Cl}^{-}(\mathrm{l}) \longrightarrow \mathrm{Cl}_2(\mathrm{~g})+2 \mathrm{e}^{-}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A black mineral on roasting breaks up into two compounds $(X)$ and $(Y)$ with the liberation of a gas $(Z)$. When air is passed through the molten mixture of $(\mathrm{X})$ and $(\mathrm{Y})$, both of them are converted into their respective oxides. One of them reduces its own oxide on further heating. Identify the mineral.
questions.solution:
The given black mineral is chalcopyrites which breaks up into $\mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{~S}$. FeS and $\mathrm{SO}_2$ on roasting.
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2 \mathrm{CuFeS}_2+\mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow \underset{\left(\mathrm{Xi}_1\right.}{\mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{~S}}+\underset{(\mathrm{Y})}{2 \mathrm{FeS}_1}+\underset{(7)}{\mathrm{SO}} \\
& 2 \mathrm{Cu}_2+3 \mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{O}+2 \mathrm{SO}_2 \\
& 2 \mathrm{FeS}_2+3 \mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{FeO}_2+2 \mathrm{SO}_2 \\
& \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{~S}+2 \mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 6 \mathrm{Cu}+\mathrm{SO}_2
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
